'''Zeami''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Near-Human]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commander]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Near-Human]], Zeami was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]. A member of a [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|Initiate clan]] under the instruction of [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], Zeami befriended fellow [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiate]] [[Iguni]] at a young age. Selected as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] by a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], Zeami constructed a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] which she would wield in battle.<ref name="EE" />

When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Zeami took up the rank of [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Commander]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. During the final year of the war, Zeami was stationed on Coruscant when [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] launched a [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|surprise attack]] on the capital world. Alongside Padawans Iguni and [[Zoi]], Zeami rushed out to join in the fray despite orders to remain at the Temple after being convinced by Iguni it was the right thing to do. Together with her peers, Zeami helped take out several [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]], [[Octuptarra combat tri-droid/Legends|Octuptarra combat tri-droids]] and at least one [[DSD1 dwarf spider droid/Legends|DSD1 dwarf spider droid]] before both she and Zoi were injured. Upon seeing his friend injured, Iguni took hold of her limp body and created a [[Telekinesis/Legends|whirlwind]] in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] which surrounded the two of them and damaged everything nearby, including Zoi.<ref name="EE" />

[[File:Zeami_Hurt.jpg|thumb|left|Zeami, injured during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]].]]

It was at this point that Iguni's [[Jedi Master/Legends|master]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], arrived and ordered Iguni to return to the Temple and take his friends with him, as he was causing more harm than good. Zeami was then taken to the [[Halls of Healing]], where she was treated for her wounds. Iguni soon visited her to apologize for his actions and, though she forgave him, he vowed to grow stronger in the Force so that he could protect his friends. Zeami went back to her training along with her friends, where both she and Zoi would often check up on Iguni as he practiced his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] techniques with Master Windu. It was soon after this that Iguni had a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] killing many Jedi and turning to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="EE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zeami only appears in the story ''[[Evil Eyes]]'', published in ''[[Star Wars Manga: Black]]'' issue of [[Tokyopop's Star Wars Manga]]. Originally published in Japan, those stories were considered to be of "fuzzy" continuity by Lucasfilm.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=980#15895005|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-11-06|time=6:03 PM}}</ref> Since then, they have been republished in English in the United Kingdom under [[Infinities]] label, which established the material in them as [[Canon|non-canonical]].
